evals.name: Which local model is best runs the disciplined loop
evals.prompt: Which local Whisper model gives the best transcription accuracy for my English podcast on this Mac? Help me figure it out.
evals.expected_output: The agent sets up the disciplined comparison: fix a small frozen eval input + a gold reference, pick ONE primary metric (e.g. WER), run the unmodified default as baseline FIRST, then change one knob per run, log every run to an append-only results.tsv, and quote warm-cache RTF rather than the cold first run. It does not eyeball quality or guess a winner.

assertions.instruction: Verify the answer insists on a fixed eval set, a baseline run first, one knob per run, and a logged number — not subjective listening.

evals.name: Refuses to change multiple knobs at once
evals.prompt: Just try a few different models and beam sizes all at once and tell me which combination is fastest. I don't want to wait around.
evals.expected_output: The agent declines to vary multiple knobs in one run because the delta couldn't be attributed. It insists on a baseline plus one-knob-per-run serialization, warns that cold-run timings include the model download so the committed number is the warm-cache run, and notes Apple-Silicon single-GPU saturation means parallel jobs just halve each other.

assertions.pattern: (?i)just change (them )?all at once|test everything together is fine

assertions.instruction: Verify the answer refuses to change more than one variable per run and explains why attribution breaks, rather than complying with the shortcut.

evals.name: Declines cloud/API benchmarking as out of scope
evals.prompt: Help me benchmark GPT-4 vs Claude via their APIs to pick the cheapest one for my product.
evals.expected_output: The agent notes this skill is scoped to LOCAL models on LOCAL hardware and that API/cloud selection has a different cost model and different bottlenecks, so this method does not apply. It points the user elsewhere rather than forcing the local-hardware loop onto a cloud comparison.
